title: Form 10 — Answer
subtitle: Ontario Family Court — Family Law Rules (FLR 10)
helpIntro: Form 10 is the Answer — it is how you formally respond when someone has filed a court application against you (Form 8). You use it to agree with or disagree with what the applicant is asking for, and to ask for your own orders from the court. You must serve your Answer on the applicant and file it with the court within 30 days of being served with the Application.

parts.title: Court information
parts.subtitle: Step 1 of 7
parts.intro: Enter the court file information from the Application (Form 8) you received.

fields.label: Courthouse
options: St. Catharines — Superior Court of Justice
Thunder Bay — Superior Court of Justice

fields.label: Court file number (from the Application you received)
fields.helpText: This is printed on the Form 8 Application that was served on you.

fields.label: Date printed on the Application you received
fields.helpText: Found at the top of the Form 8 Application.

parts.title: Your information (the respondent)
parts.subtitle: Step 2 of 7
parts.intro: You are the respondent — the person responding to the application. Fill in your details.

fields.label: Your full legal name
fields.helpText: In the Answer form, you are called the 'Respondent'. Your name auto-fills from your profile.

fields.label: Your date of birth
Your address for service
fields.helpText: This is where court documents can be delivered to you.

fields.label: City
Province
Postal code
Phone number
Email address
Do you have a lawyer representing you?
Your lawyer's full name
Your lawyer's address
Your lawyer's phone number

parts.title: The other party (the applicant)
parts.subtitle: Step 3 of 7
parts.intro: Confirm the details of the person who filed the Application against you.

fields.label: The applicant's full legal name
The applicant's address for service
Does the applicant have a lawyer?
Applicant's lawyer's name
Applicant's lawyer's address

parts.title: Your response to the Application
parts.subtitle: Step 4 of 7
parts.intro: Go through each thing the applicant is asking for and say whether you agree or disagree. You can also ask for your own orders in the next step.

fields.label: Overall, what is your position on the Application?
options: I disagree with everything the applicant is asking for
I agree with some things and disagree with others
I agree with everything the applicant is asking for
I agree with most things but want to add my own requests

fields.label: Do you agree with the applicant's request about custody / decision-making?
options: I have my own proposal (describe below)

fields.label: Describe your position on custody / decision-making
fields.placeholder: e.g. I disagree. I am the primary caregiver and the children should reside primarily with me. I propose that both parents share decision-making but that the children live with me.
fields.helpText: Be specific. What arrangement do you propose instead?

fields.label: Do you agree with the applicant's request about parenting time / access?
options: I have my own proposal (describe below)

fields.label: Describe your proposed parenting time schedule
fields.placeholder: e.g. I propose that the children live with me week-on, week-off. The Applicant would have parenting time every other weekend.

fields.label: Do you agree with the applicant's request about child support?
options: I have my own proposal (describe below)

fields.label: Describe your position on child support
fields.placeholder: e.g. I disagree with the amount requested. Based on my income of $X per year, the correct amount under the Child Support Guidelines is $Y per month.

fields.label: Do you agree with the applicant's request about spousal support?
Describe your position on spousal support
fields.placeholder: e.g. I disagree. We were together for only 2 years and I should not be required to pay spousal support.

fields.label: Do you agree with the applicant's request about property / equalization?
Describe your position on property or equalization
fields.placeholder: e.g. I disagree with the applicant's valuation of the matrimonial home. The correct value is $X not $Y.

fields.label: Is there anything else in the Application you disagree with? Explain.
fields.placeholder: e.g. I dispute the applicant's claim that I was the primary breadwinner. I was laid off in January 2024.

parts.title: Orders you are asking for
parts.subtitle: Step 5 of 7
parts.intro: If you want orders of your own — not just to respond to the applicant's requests — list them here. This is your chance to ask the court for what YOU want.

fields.label: Are you asking the court for any orders of your own?
fields.helpText: Even if the applicant is the one who started the case, you can use your Answer to ask for orders that benefit you.

fields.label: Are you asking for custody / decision-making responsibility?
Are you asking for a specific parenting time schedule?
Are you asking the other party to pay child support to you?
Are you asking the other party to pay spousal support to you?
Are you asking for a property / equalization payment?
Are you asking for a restraining or non-harassment order?
Any other orders (describe below)?
Describe all the orders you are asking for in detail
fields.placeholder:
e.g. 1. An order that I have primary decision-making responsibility for our child Alex Lance, born April 12, 2015.
2. An order that the Applicant pay child support of $X per month pursuant to the Child Support Guidelines.
3. An order that the Applicant pay costs of this proceeding.
fields.helpText: Number each order. Be specific — include names, amounts, and dates where possible.

parts.title: Children (if applicable)
parts.subtitle: Step 6 of 7
parts.intro: If children are involved in this case, provide their details. The court needs this information to assess their best interests.

fields.label: Are there children involved in this case?
List the children (name, date of birth, where they currently live)
fields.placeholder:
Alex Lance — born April 12, 2015 — living with me at 123 Main Street, Toronto
Sam Lance — born September 3, 2018 — living with me at 123 Main Street, Toronto
fields.helpText: One child per line: full name, date of birth, and where they currently live.

fields.label: Describe where the children have been living since the parents separated
fields.placeholder: e.g. Since our separation in March 2024, both children have lived primarily with me. The Applicant sees them every other weekend.

fields.label: Why is your proposal in the best interests of the children?
fields.placeholder: e.g. I am the primary caregiver. I take the children to school, medical appointments, and activities. They have a stable home with me and are enrolled in school nearby.
fields.helpText: The court always focuses on what is best for the children — not what is best for either parent.

parts.title: Review and sign
parts.subtitle: Step 7 of 7
parts.intro: Review your Answer. Once filed, serve a copy on the applicant (or their lawyer) within 30 days of being served with the Application. Then file proof of service (Form 6B).

fields.label: Is there anything else important the court should know that you haven't mentioned?
fields.placeholder: e.g. There is a prior agreement between the parties dated January 2023 that the Applicant has not disclosed.

fields.label: Date you are signing this Answer
I confirm the information in this Answer is accurate and complete to the best of my knowledge.
